title: Service Overview Report
description: This Workflow generates Service Performance reports and emails to the specified addresses.
guide:
# Service Overview Report
Get a list of your top Services. This Workflow queries Grail and provides data to Dynatrace Assist to get recommendations. These recommendations are then sent to email(s) of your choice.

# Setup
1. Change emails (`to`, `cc`, `bcc`) to be an array of strings.
2. Test with a manual Run, by click the `Run` button.
3. If everything works as expected, change the [Trigger](?trigger=) to a schedule, e.g. Weekly, Daily, etc.

# More advanced interactions
Consider using our MCP server, [local](https://github.com/dynatrace-oss/dynatrace-mcp) or [remote](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/discover-dynatrace/platform/davis-ai/dynatrace-mcp ).

service_overview.name: service_overview

input.query:
timeseries {latency_p50 = median(dt.service.request.response_time),
           latency_p90 = percentile(dt.service.request.response_time, 90),
           latency_p99 = percentile(dt.service.request.response_time, 99),
           requests = sum(dt.service.request.count), 
           errors = sum(dt.service.request.failure_count), from: @d-1d, to: @d},
           by:{dt.entity.service}
| lookup [ timeseries latency_avg = avg(dt.service.request.response_time),
           by:{dt.entity.service}],
            sourceField:dt.entity.service, lookupField:dt.entity.service,
            prefix: "latencyAvg."
| lookup [ timeseries http_5xx = sum(dt.service.request.count,default: 0.0),
           by:{dt.entity.service},
           filter: (http.response.status_code >= 500 and http.response.status_code <= 599)],
            sourceField:dt.entity.service, lookupField:dt.entity.service,
            prefix: "http5xx."
| lookup [ timeseries http_4xx = sum(dt.service.request.count,default: 0.0),
           by:{dt.entity.service},
           filter: (http.response.status_code >= 400 and http.response.status_code <= 499)],
            sourceField:dt.entity.service, lookupField:dt.entity.service,
            prefix: "http4xx."
| fieldsAdd Latency_Avg = arrayAvg(latencyAvg.latency_avg),
            Latency_p50 = arrayAvg(latency_p50),
            Latency_p90 = arrayAvg(latency_p90),
            Latency_p99 = arrayAvg(latency_p99),
            Requests = arraySum(requests), 
            Failures = arraySum(errors),
            Errors500 = arraySum(http5xx.http_5xx),
            Errors400 = arraySum(http4xx.http_4xx)
| fieldsAdd FailureRate = (Failures/Requests) *100
| fieldsAdd Service = entityName(dt.entity.service)
| fields Service,
         Requests,
         Latency_Avg,
         Latency_p50,
         Latency_p90,
         Latency_p99,
         FailureRate,
         `Failures` = if(Failures == 0,"0", else:concat("[",Failures,"](https://","{{environment().id}}",".apps.dynatrace.com/ui/apps/dynatrace.services/failure-analysis?filter=dt.entity.service+%3D+",dt.entity.service,"+AND+request.status_code+%3D+Failure&tf=-1d%40d%3B%40d")),
         `5xx` = if(isNull(Errors500), "0", else:concat("[",round(Errors500,decimals:0),"](https://","{{environment().id}}",".apps.dynatrace.com/ui/apps/dynatrace.distributedtracing/explorer?filter=dt.entity.service+%3D+",dt.entity.service,"+AND+http.response.status_code+>%3D+500+AND+http.response.status_code+<%3D+599&tf=-1d%40d%3B%40d")),
         `4xx` = if(isNull(Errors400), "0", else:concat("[",round(Errors400,decimals:0),"](https://","{{environment().id}}",".apps.dynatrace.com/ui/apps/dynatrace.distributedtracing/explorer?filter=dt.entity.service+%3D+",dt.entity.service,"+AND+http.response.status_code+>%3D+400+AND+http.response.status_code+<%3D+499&tf=-1d%40d%3B%40d"))

| sort Requests desc
| limit 25

service_overview.description: Make use of Dynatrace Grail data in your workflow.

service_overview_report_prompt.name: service_overview_report_prompt

input.prompt:
Provide a report for the following use case for the top 25 Services:
## Service Overview Report
input.instruction:
Instead of bulleted list, format all Services in a 25 row table. 
Provide the full table.
Provide Notes for each service in the table.
Also provide an overall Summary, Observations, Recommendations and Next Steps below the table.
Make sure to include `Failures`, `5xx` and `4xx` links as columns in the table.
Order the columns of the table as follows: 
Service, Requests, Latency_Avg, Latency_p50, Latency_p90, Latency_p99, FailureRate, Failures, 5xx, 4xx and Notes.
Latency_Avg is in ms.
input.supplementary:
Use this analysis for the complete list of top 25 Services:
{{result("service_overview")["records"]}}

service_overview_report_prompt.description: Prompt the Dynatrace Intelligence generative AI
